backend_integration.overview: How to read backend code and translate it into frontend-safe TypeScript. This file covers: Python→TypeScript type mapping, API client patterns, Vite proxy config, error handling contracts, and session management. Every pattern here is derived from the FastAPI + Pydantic + LangGraph backend generated by multi-agent-system-builder mode.

type_mapping.description: Map Pydantic model fields from backend/app/models/schemas.py to TypeScript interfaces. Read the actual schemas file — do not guess field names.

api_client_pattern.description: One API client file at src/lib/api.ts. One typed async function per endpoint. Uses the Fetch API — no Axios dependency needed. Base URL comes from Vite env var, defaulting to empty string (uses Vite proxy in dev).

vite_proxy_config.description: Vite proxies /api/* to the backend in development, eliminating CORS issues. Read the backend CORS allowed_origins and the backend port from main.py. Default backend port: 8000.
vite_proxy_config.docker_note:
In Docker Compose, the frontend container reaches the backend via the service name, not localhost. Update the proxy target based on environment:
Dev (local, no Docker): target: 'http://localhost:8000' Dev (Docker Compose): target: 'http://backend:8000'
The cleanest approach — read from env var in vite.config.ts: target: process.env.VITE_BACKEND_URL ?? 'http://localhost:8000'
Add to frontend/.env.local (local dev): VITE_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:8000 Add to docker-compose.yml frontend service environment: environment: - VITE_BACKEND_URL=http://backend:8000

custom_hooks_pattern.description: One custom hook per data concern. Hooks handle loading, error, and data states. Components receive data — they never call API functions directly.

session_management.description: The backend uses thread_id (sent as session_id in ChatRequest) to scope conversation history per user session. The frontend must generate and persist a session_id, then pass it on every chat request.
session_management.note: Use sessionStorage (not localStorage) so each browser tab gets its own session. This prevents one tab's conversation leaking into another tab's context.

streaming_pattern.description: If the backend has a streaming endpoint (StreamingResponse / SSE), use the browser's EventSource API or ReadableStream from fetch. Do NOT use a standard fetch — it will buffer the full response before resolving.

error_states.description: Every component that fetches data must handle three states: loading, error, and success. Never show a blank screen on error.

required_states.state: Show a loading indicator using the active style system's pattern
Show the error message in a styled alert component
Show a meaningful empty state — never just nothing

error_states.backend_error_format: FastAPI returns errors as: { "detail": "error message string" } The ApiError class in api.ts extracts this. Always display err.message to the user.

env_file.template: # frontend/.env.example VITE_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:8000 # Local dev without Docker # VITE_BACKEND_URL=http://backend:8000 # Docker Compose (set in docker-compose.yml instead) VITE_API_BASE_URL= # Leave empty — proxy handles routing VITE_API_KEY= # Set if backend API_KEY auth is enabled
